prompt: The parent class fields are not restored after deserialization. What is the bug?
choices: Child cannot implement Serializable if Base does not
When a Serializable subclass extends a non-Serializable parent, the parent's fields are not saved; on deserialization the parent's no-arg constructor is called, resetting those fields to defaults
int fields in non-Serializable parents are always serialized as 0
Serializable must be implemented by all classes in the hierarchy
explanation: A Serializable class can extend a non-Serializable class, but the parent's state is not saved. On deserialization, Java calls the first non-Serializable ancestor's no-arg constructor. Base's no-arg constructor resets baseVal to 10 (default), but any mutations are lost. If Base has no no-arg constructor, deserialization throws InvalidClassException.
